Conclusion: The Question Remains Unanswered, Potential Alignment with Party Lines

macro_segments.title: Drawn Water and the Eighteen Decrees: The Gezeirah Against Bathing After Immersion in Turgid Mikvah Water

micro_segments.title: The Case of Head and Majority Immersed in Drawn Water: Why Tamei After Immersion?
Rashi's Explanation: Tamei for Terumah Until Another Immersion, and the Eighteen Decrees

Extension of the Gezeirah: Three Lugin of Drawn Water Poured on One Is Equivalent
Rami bar Abba's Question: Half from Immersion, Half from Pouring—Is the Gezeirah Applied?

macro_segments.title: Takanat Ezra and the Ba'al Keri: Purification Through Nine Kabin of Drawn Water for the Sick

Ezra's Decree: Requiring Immersion Before Torah Study to Ensure Serious Demeanor

Modern Application: The Takana Is No Longer Observed, Commentary on Contemporary Seriousness

Practical Application for Temple Mount Access and Conversion Immersion
Rav Papa's Extension: Half-Immersion, Half-Pouring in the Context of Ba'al Keri Purification

macro_segments.title: Testimony for the Get: Witnesses to Writing and Sealing in the Case of Foreign-Brought Documents

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah Cases: One Witness Saw Writing, Another Saw Sealing—Is the Get Valid?

Two Messengers Principle: If Both Are Appointed, the Get Is Valid Without the Testimony Requirement

macro_segments.title: Rabbah's Alternative: The Lishma Principle as the Sole Operating Basis Across All Positions

macro_segments.title: Resolving the Baraita: Rabbi Yehuda's Disagreement Applies Only to Cases with Multiple Witnesses
